title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum is set to play against the Washington Wizards after being left off Boston's injury report for Saturday.
Tatum missed Thursday's game due to right Achilles injury management after three straight starts since his season debut.
Baylor Scheierman is probable with right ankle sprain, and Derrick White is probable with right knee contusion for Saturday.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's 'War Machine' stars Alan Ritchson as a soldier handling PTSD, available now.
Disney+'s 'Zootopia 2' features Nick and Judy solving mysteries in the animal city.
HBO Max's comedy 'Rooster' with Steve Carell explores campus life after divorce.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots signed offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker from the Jets to strengthen their offensive line.
Vera-Tucker, 26, has started 43 games but missed last season due to triceps injury.
Morgan Moses influenced Vera-Tucker's decision to join the Patriots during free agency.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora names Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as leaders for the 2026 Boston Red Sox team.
Trevor Story, entering his 11th MLB season, led Red Sox in home runs and RBIs in 2025.
Garrett Crochet, Red Sox ace, led MLB in strikeouts last season; compared to legendary pitchers by Cora.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC will debut in the NWSL against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
Head coach Filipa Patão and GM Domènec Guasch aim for an attacking, possession-based style in their inaugural season.
Gotham FC, the reigning NWSL champions, features stars like Rose Lavelle and poses a challenging first matchup for Legacy.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports a bill to stop the twice-yearly clock change, citing lifestyle disruptions.
The proposed legislation, filed by State Senator John F. Keenan, suggests adopting a permanent Atlantic Standard Time.
Research links clock changes to increased heart attacks and strokes, influencing the debate on ending the practice statewide.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announces the 2026 Pride parade and festival will take place on June 6.
The parade will start at Copley Square at 11 a.m. and conclude at Boston Common with a concert and festival.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the event's inclusivity, and volunteer recruitment details are available on the organization's website.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl was released from ICE custody after being detained in Marlborough, Massachusetts.
The girl, found with alleged gang members, was not identified as having legal guardians at the time of detention.
A federal judge ordered her release, and she was reunited with her aunt in Boston; a court appearance is pending.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are facing a scoring slump, impacting their playoff hopes as they hold the final wild-card spot.
David Pastrnak leads with seven points in eight games, but struggles persist at 5-on-5 play for the team's top players.
A lineup reshuffle is being considered to improve performance, with Pastrnak potentially paired with younger players Minten and Khusnutdinov.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss visiting researcher at Harvard Medical School, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Cogne aims to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center at Brigham and Women's Hospital.
Cogne's research focuses on neurorehabilitation, helping trauma survivors regain movement and independence.
